steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The subject is the plural noun phrase “emulsifiers such as stearyl alcohol,” followed by the infinitive phrase “to blend oil and water...” The blank requires a finite verb that agrees with this plural subject.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
The rule is subject-verb agreement: a plural subject requires a plural verb. Are employed is the correct plural verb form; it matches “emulsifiers” and creates the standard pattern “are employed to [infinitive].” Choices A and B lack the plural verb conjugation required for agreement, and D introduces a relative clause with an unclear pronoun.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
